findings.description: The `do1` method is omitted from the code example. Its inclusion in the source is significant because it shows that `setViewDataToRequestScope()` is called both on initial display AND on error recovery — demonstrating the shared-method extraction pattern. Without `do1`, an AI assistant cannot fully reconstruct why the method is private and shared.
findings.source_evidence: Source includes `do1` calling `setViewDataToRequestScope()` before returning the input screen, establishing the pattern that the same helper is reused in both normal and error flows.

findings.description: PascalCase class names `HttpResponse`, `HttpRequest`, and `ExecutionContext` appear in the code example but are absent from the hints array.
findings.source_evidence: Source code declares `public HttpResponse do1(HttpRequest req, ExecutionContext ctx)` and `public HttpResponse do2(HttpRequest req, ExecutionContext ctx)` — all three are PascalCase class names present in the content.
